articles.headline: This Underrated KFC Menu Item 'Doesn't Even Taste Like Fast Food'
articles.author: Julia Holland
articles.summary: KFC is one of the oldest fast food fried chicken chains and has established itself as a classic in the U.S. and beyond. While KFC keeps things fresh with new deals and menu items, it also has tried-and-true classics with a loyal fanbase. According to this fanbase, customers are sleeping on one of the most underrated KFC menu items: the chicken pot pie. KFC's chicken pot pie features a filling of peas, carrots, shredded chicken, and potatoes in a thick gravy sauce under a flaky crust. As per KFC regulars on Reddit, it "doesn't even taste like fast food." One fan even claimed that "there isn't a better single tasting item at a fast food mega chain" and that it's more akin to "something someone's grandma made." Multiple customers have echoed the sentiment. One Redditor described it as a "crust [that] is light and flaky in a really good way. The chicken and vegetables are balanced in a delicious gravy." Customers have also complimented the crust-to-filling ratio, breaking up the top crust to mix it into the filling for a well-rounded bite every time.

articles.headline: Authorities Investigate Taco Bell And Lettuce In Multistate Cyclosporiasis Outbreak
articles.author: Lena H. Sun
articles.summary: Federal and state health officials are investigating whether Taco Bell restaurants played a role in one of the largest U.S. outbreaks of a gastrointestinal illness caused by a parasite that contaminates fresh produce, according to two individuals familiar with the investigation. The two individuals spoke on the condition of anonymity because the investigation is ongoing into the outbreak of the infection that can cause days of diarrhea and has sickened thousands. Notices posted at some Detroit-area Taco Bell restaurants last week told customers the chain was “currently unable to sell Lettuce, Cilantro Onion, Pico de Gallo, and Guacamole due to a nationwide recall,” according to media reports. Authorities have not yet identified a specific produce grower or supplier responsible for the multistate outbreak of cyclosporiasis that has hospitalized more than 80 people. Public health officials say there could be multiple culprits.

articles.headline: Chipotle Backs Innovators Transforming Food Systems
articles.author: Brooke Just
articles.summary: Six young companies have landed backing from Chipotle Mexican Grill’s Cultivate Next venture fund as the restaurant chain looks to seed new ideas across agriculture, sustainability, supply chains and restaurant technology. The companies that received investments include: Benchmark Labs, founded by Carlos Gaitan, develops artificial intelligence (AI)-powered weather forecasting and climate intelligence technology by combining environmental data, machine learning and predictive analytics to help agriculture operators make more informed decisions, the company said. IMIO, founded by Charles Smith, said it develops microbial agricultural solutions designed to improve soil health, crop performance and farm productivity to reduce dependence on conventional chemical inputs.

articles.headline: Will Tim Hortons And Burger King Momentum Shift Restaurant Brands International's (QSR) Diversified Portfolio Narrative
articles.author: Sasha Jovanovic
articles.summary: Restaurant Brands International recently attracted fresh attention after Tim Hortons extended its comparable sales momentum and Burger King reported improved operating performance across its system. This combination points to healthier fundamentals in two of the company’s largest banners, reinforcing the benefits of its diversified quick-service portfolio. Next, we’ll examine how Tim Hortons’ sustained comparable sales momentum could influence Restaurant Brands International’s broader investment narrative and outlook. Invest in the nuclear renaissance through our list of 89 elite nuclear energy infrastructure plays powering the global AI revolution.

articles.headline: McDonald's Has A New Sauce Coming To Its Menu
articles.author: Mike Snider
articles.summary: McDonald's has a saucy menu option coming soon: Caesar sauce. The fast-food chain will begin offering McDonald's Caesar sauce on July 21 as a limited-time option on chicken menu items. The new Caesar sauce is described as "a creamy, garlicky parmesan blend infused with notes of lemon," according to McDonald's Menu Spotter site. Back in June, McDonald's began offering a FIFA World Cup 26 Meal with one of nine collectible cups featuring soccer stars such as current USMNT's Christian Pulisic, Spain's Lamine Yamal, Mexico's Santiago Gimenez, French superstar now Fox analyst Thierry Henry, LAFC forward Son Heung-min, David Beckham and others – including Grimace.

articles.headline: Pizzeria Buffet-Inspired QSR Menus
articles.author: Michael Hemsworth
articles.summary: The Pizza Hut Throwback Value Menu is a new offering from the QSR brand that's inspired by its previous dining experiences to take aim at Millennial nostalgia in a cost-effective way. The menu encourages consumers to build their own Pizza Hut buffet experience by mixing and matching various items that can be conveniently ordered to their house. The menu is competitively priced as a nod to increasing calls for low-cost dining options with prices starting at $3 to encourage customers to order multiples of their favorites. The Pizza Hut Throwback Value Menu is being launched with the Back to the Hut digital experience to quiz consumers on their brand knowledge to unlock rewards and more. Both announcements come as part of the nostalgia marketing surge that's meant to capture the excitement consumers have about their childhood experiences with their favorite brands.

articles.headline: How Food Waste Tracking Can Unlock Stronger Restaurant Margins
articles.summary: For quick-service restaurant operators, performance is measured in seconds and cents. Operators track ticket times, labor ratios and food costs down to the unit. Yet one of the most consistent drivers of margin loss has received far less attention: surplus food from plates, preparation scraps, overpurchasing and more. That is beginning to change. As inflation, supply volatility and labor pressures continue to challenge profitability, operators are reexamining what gets thrown away and why. What they are finding is that these losses are not random. Surplus food is measurable, patterned and in many cases, preventable. In the United States, ReFED estimates that restaurants generate an estimated 11.4 million tons of food waste each year, representing roughly $25 billion in lost value. For QSR brands operating hundreds or thousands of units, even small reductions translate into meaningful gains.

articles.headline: Gusto! Introducing Adders And Snack To Menu
articles.summary: Atlanta-based fast-casual brand gusto! is introducing Adders and Snacks, two new menu offerings that give guests more choice and more occasions to enjoy the brand. Available beginning July 14 at all 13 metro Atlanta locations, the launch marks the first time guests can customize gusto!’s signature bowls and wraps, alongside a new lineup of snacks for today’s more flexible eating occasions. For the first time in gusto!’s 11-year history, guests can customize their favorite bowls and wraps with Adders—a collection of fresh ingredients that lets every guest put their own fresh twist on the flavors they love. At the same time, the Snacks menu introduces a new way to enjoy gusto!, with four menu items designed for everything from post-workout cravings and afternoon pick-me-ups to those moments when a full meal is more than you need.

articles.headline: Sauce-Focused Fried Chicken Menus
articles.author: Michael Hemsworth
articles.summary: The McDonald's Caesar menu is a new series of items headed to the QSR brand's lineup for a limited time only to help satisfy customer interest in sauce-driven fried chicken items. The menu puts the brand's Caesar-style dipping sauce in the spotlight that boasts a creamy Parmesan blend base with notes of garlic and lemon throughout. The menu features the Bacon Caesar McCrispy, Caesar Snack Wrap and the McCrispy Strips with Caesar Sauce, which are each part of the brand's McCrispy platform to complement the new sauce. The McDonald's Caesar menu helps the QSR brand to catch the attention of fried chicken fans and also help to expand on its existing category prowess for a newstalgia twist that works well for foodies.

articles.headline: KFC Vs Chick-Fil-A: Which Serves A Healthier Classic Chicken Sandwich?
articles.author: Kashmir Brummel
articles.summary: If you're looking for a healthy option for lunch or dinner, a classic chicken sandwich probably won't be top of your list. But if you do have a craving that nothing else will satisfy, it's not necessarily off-limits; it might just be about making the best choice. On paper, KFC and Chick-fil-A have fairly similar offerings: a fried chicken filet and a couple of pickle slices on a bun, with KFC opting for mayo, while the Chick-fil-A bun is buttered. Despite this, there are some significant nutritional differences between the two. Before making your choice, you need to consider what makes food healthy or unhealthy? Generally speaking, healthy foods contain higher levels of beneficial nutrients such as protein and fiber and lower levels of saturated fat, sodium, or added sugar. Calorie count isn't necessarily an indicator of whether something is healthy or not, as long as you're not eating more calories than you need.

articles.headline: KFC Puts Iconic Cult-Classic Item Back On The Menu After Fans Demand Return: ‘Times Are Healing’
articles.author: Ben Cost
articles.summary: KFC is catering to nosh-talgic fast food fans by dusting off a cult classic dish from the late 20th century — popcorn chicken. On Monday, July 13, the red and white chicken chain started serving the crispy protein nuggets, marking a triumphant return for the fan-favorite food, per a press release. Originally debuting in the 1990s, Popcorn Chicken graced KFC menus until it was discontinued in 2023. By that point, this donut hole-ified fried chicken had earned a massive cult following, with the dish’s diehards flocking to social media to demand its return. So, by pop-ular demand, the chicken conglomerate decided to bring it back. “For years, KFC has heard directly from fans clamoring for Popcorn Chicken’s return,” said Melissa Cash, Chief Marketing Officer, KFC US. “We read every comment, every DM, and even every online petition signature, and the message was impossible to ignore.”

articles.headline: Health Officials Investigate Possible Taco Bell Lettuce Link To Cyclosporiasis Outbreak, Report Says
articles.author: Naina Srivastava
articles.summary: Public health officials are investigating whether lettuce served at Taco Bell restaurants contributed to the outbreak of an intestinal infection, cyclosporiasis, the Washington Post reported Tuesday, citing unnamed sources. Federal officials said on Tuesday that infection patterns may differ by location, and that they believe cases in four states — Michigan, Ohio, Kentucky and West Virginia — are linked. Michigan has been hit hardest by the outbreak, tallying over 3,309 cases, according to the Michigan Department of Health and Human Services. Some who contracted the illness told investigators they had eaten at Taco Bell, a person familiar with the national investigation told the Post. But others who became sick had not eaten there. Taco Bell locations in the Detroit area posted notices that told customers they were “currently unable to sell Lettuce, Cilantro Onion, Pico de Gallo, and Guacamole due to a nationwide recall."

articles.headline: State Prosecutors Say Charges Altered In Food Adulteration Case At Upstate Wendy's
articles.author: Zach Rainey, Chloe McCoy
articles.summary: We are learning more about an alleged food adulterating case at an Upstate South Carolina Wendy's that resulted in the arrest of three employees. Back in June, three employees at the Wendy's in Union were charged with tampering with a food product following an incident that happened on May 30. In that incident, a customer reported to law enforcement that an employee advised her not to eat the food she had just purchased from the restaurant. According to the report, the employee told the customer that the food had been placed in a trash can, retrieved, spit on, and then rewrapped before being given to her. The solicitor's office said they completed their review of the incident on Wednesday, July 15, and concluded the most appropriate charge was adulteration of food, rather than food product tampering.

articles.headline: Fast Food Chain Continues Central Kentucky Expansion With New Restaurant Opening
articles.author: Brian Simms
articles.summary: A new-to-Richmond fast food chain is opening in the Central Kentucky town. Chipotle Mexican Grill is opening its first location in Richmond at 10:45 a.m. on Thursday, July 16. The new restaurant is located at 863 Eastern Bypass, near Hobby Lobby and Menards. The fast-casual restaurant chain specializes in bowls, tacos and burritos made to order in front of the customer. The new location is part of a massive expansion of Chipotle restaurants in recent years in Central Kentucky. New locations have opened or opening soon in Lexington, Georgetown, Frankfort and Nicholasville. The new Richmond location will feature a “Chipotlane,” a drive-thru pick-up lane that allows guests to pick up digital orders without leaving their cars. It will also have the restaurant’s new high protein menu with dishes that range from 15 to 81 grams of protein per item.

articles.headline: Chipotle’s Latest Expansion Plan Comes With Real Risk
articles.author: Tony Owusu
articles.summary: I remember the first time I brought my Mexican girlfriend to Chipotle (CMG). She had never heard of the fast food restaurant. Unlike Taco Bell, which seemingly has a mythical status in Mexico (though there are no locations there at the moment), Chipotle isn’t very popular south of the border. “Taco Bell? Chipotle blows Taco Bell out of the water,” I assured her and her family. But the truth was that I actually hadn’t eaten at Chipotle in years. If all goes well for Chipotle now, my girlfriend’s family won’t have to come to the U.S. to try the food. Chipotle announced that it will open its inaugural store in the Monterrey area on Thursday with plans to open other locations in the state of Nuevo Leon later in the year.

articles.headline: New Look For Burger King In Lufkin Promises A Fresh Dining Vibe
articles.author: Danny Merrell
articles.summary: The innovators at Burger King recently introduced its new 'Sizzle' concept, the company's latest attempt to freshen the brand and deliver a more appealing dining experience for its customers. Don't worry, their signature flame-grilled burgers are not on the chopping block. This is all part of the company's 'Reclaim the Flame' initiative, which was introduced in 2023. Since that time, numerous BK locations have been renovated to Sizzle standards, with many more upgrades on the way. This is much more than a coat of paint on a fading wall. According to a post on BurgerBeast.com, this complete redesign will incorporate "double-lane drive-thrus, digital kiosks, open-flame grills visible to customers, and interiors that feel closer to fast-casual than fast food." Some new menu items and options may also be a possibility.

articles.headline: Burger King Brings Back A Retro Chicken Favorite
articles.author: Moz Farooque
articles.summary: Fast-food menus continue to get louder as chains try to capture the attention of younger consumers whose noses are often buried in their phones. I still love the McDonald’s Big Mac because its appeal is straightforward: two beef patties, a familiar sauce, and a taste that has barely needed reinvention. Some of my younger friends, though, want more oomph. They gravitate toward oversized patties, hotter sauces, and increasingly aggressive flavor combinations. I’m not from the TikTok generation, so I don’t always “get it,” but the best fast food often comes down to a few recognizable ingredients prepared consistently. Burger King is leaning into that same idea, with a nostalgic twist. The chain is giving longtime fans a new reason to revisit it. Burger King is giving its long-running Original Chicken Sandwich two limited-time makeovers for summer, adding a Loaded Jalapeño version and a Club version at participating restaurants nationwide, according to BrandEating. The Loaded Jalapeño Original Chicken Sandwich pairs Burger King’s crispy, breaded white-meat chicken patty with creamy jalapeño sauce, crispy, pickled jalapeños, crispy onions, lettuce, tomatoes, and American cheese, all served on the chain’s familiar hoagie-style sesame-seed bun.

articles.headline: The Seriously Nostalgic Burger King Fries That Are Long Overdue For A Revival
articles.author: Rosie Govin
articles.summary: In a theoretical in-memoriam montage of discontinued Burger King items, one stands out as something that may have simply been too far ahead of its time to succeed when first released. Burger King Shake 'Em Up Fries were introduced in 2002 and consisted of French fries and a little baggie of cheese-flavored powder. You'd pour the cheese powder into the bag of fries, shake them up, and be left with cheesy, coated, flavorful spuds. In the early oughts, this proved to be a little too high-concept for Burger King customers, despite the iconic commercial. Shake 'Em Up Fries were discontinued within a year. But fast-forward a couple of decades, and fans are more than ready for their triumphant return. "Brooooo that was my childhood," one Redditor reminisced. "Go to Burger King for the Shake 'Em fries and the cream Icee. Head home and sit watching the Cartoon Network Friday Night line up."

articles.headline: The Next Generation Of QSR, Part 1: The New Economics Of Value
articles.author: Sasha Jovanovic
articles.summary: This is part one of a series. In Part 2 of The Next Generation of QSR series, we'll explore how loyalty programs, artificial intelligence and personalization are becoming the next frontier in creating value, strengthening guest relationships and driving long-term growth. According to the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics' Consumer Price Index (CPI) for Food Away From Home (restaurants and other foodservice), the cost of dining out has increased by an estimated 30% over the past five years. That means quick-service brands are rethinking the term value. Specifically, what it means for their guests, their brands and their bottom lines.

articles.headline: FDA Outlines Risk-Based Future For Food Industry Oversight
articles.author: Virginia Lee
articles.summary: Food companies with strong compliance records could face fewer routine federal inspections as the FDA redirects resources toward higher-risk facilities, complex investigations and imported foods, as noted at this week’s IFT FIRST event. Dr. Donald Prater, the FDA’s acting deputy commissioner for human foods, outlined a changing regulatory model built around state partnerships, artificial intelligence and greater access to industry data. “This is not a reduction in oversight,” Prater said. “It’s a reallocation of FDA resources toward higher-risk, more complex work.” Prater said the FDA’s seeking new ways to address acute threats such as foodborne illness alongside chronic risks involving nutrition and food ingredients.

articles.headline: Fresh Kitchen Expands Leadership Team
articles.summary: Fresh Kitchen (FK), the healthy, fast, Modern-American bowl concept, has announced two additions to its leadership team: William “Bill” Knopf as SVP, Head of Operations and Matt Livingston as Chief Development Officer. Knopf joins Fresh Kitchen following senior operations roles at honeygrow and Noodles & Company. Livingston joins after more than a decade leading real estate, asset management and market development at First Watch Restaurants. The executives will lead two functions critical to Fresh Kitchen’s next phase of growth: operating incredible restaurants and developing the real estate pipeline required to support the company’s plan of 100+ remarkable restaurants in the coming years. “Bill and Matt have helped grow some of the most respected brands in the restaurant industry, but what matters most is that they embody the FK culture,” said Tyler Azzarelli, CEO of Fresh Kitchen. “They understand that growth starts with great people and remarkable restaurant execution. Bill will raise the bar on operations and leadership, while Matt will build the development pipeline for what comes next.”

articles.headline: How Casey’s Keeps Outperforming Its C-Store And QSR Peers
articles.author: Brett Dworski
articles.summary: In a presentation to a room full of analysts and investors in New York City in late June, Darren Rebelez laid out the framework for how Casey’s General Stores intends to grow over the next three years. The plan includes adding 400 convenience stores through a combination of new builds and small-scale acquisitions, growing inside sales by the mid-single digits and increasing EBITDA at an 8%-10% compound annual growth rate. Before attendees had a chance to ask how the company would achieve those goals, Rebelez, the retailer’s president and CEO, said simply: Casey’s will keep doing what Casey’s does.

articles.headline: Soccer-Celebrating QSR Menu Promotions
articles.author: Michael Hemsworth
articles.summary: McDonald's Saved Menu has been developed as a temporary transformation of its Saver Menu that's influenced by the World Cup to build on the excitement surrounding the sporting event. The promotional menu is inspired by England's goalkeeper Jordan Pickford and honors the player's achievement as the country's most-capped player with some exclusive deals for customers who visit restaurants in Washington to claim. The menu will be on offer on July 15, 2026 from 5pm to 6pm and will entitle customers to choose one item from the Saver Menu free of charge. The promotional menu will also be supported by the goalkeeper's signature penalty prediction cheat sheet in the form of a sticker on the brand's cold drink cups. The McDonald's Saved Menu promotion tackles the regional marketing surge that has been taking place on a global scale during the 2026 FIFA World Cup to accelerate foot traffic by targeting fan demand.

articles.headline: A Surprising Restaurant Chain Outgrew Starbucks, Chipotle, And Dunkin’ Last Year
articles.author: María José Gutiérrez Chávez
articles.summary: America’s fastest-growing restaurant isn’t Starbucks, Chick-fil-A, or Chipotle. It’s a chicken wing chain that’s been opening hundreds of new locations. In a preview of QSR Magazine’s annual QSR 50, which ranks the leading fast food chains, Wingstop led the 2026 cohort of restaurants. The full report is expected to be released in August. While in past years Starbucks has topped the ranking, this year Wingstop has earned the top spot by opening 382 new locations, around 100 more than any other brand. Wingstop has risen through the ranks, becoming the fourth-fastest-growing brand in 2024 and reaching the top spot in just under two years. At the time, Starbucks was ahead by 589 locations, but Wingstop has since caught up.

articles.headline: Krispy Kreme Turns 89, Rolls Out 89-Cent Dozen
articles.summary: Krispy Kreme will celebrate the brand's 89th birthday Friday, July 17, by offering 89-cent Original Glazed doughnuts by the dozen, the company announced in a news release. On Friday only, fans can score an Original Glazed dozen for just 89 cents with the purchase of any dozen at regular price. The promotion will run at participating U.S. locations. The limit is two dozen in-shop and at the drive-thru, while customers can purchase an extra one dozen for online pickup or delivery. "Every Krispy Kreme birthday is really a celebration of our fans," Alison Holder, Krispy Kreme chief brand and product officer, said in the press release. "Offering Original Glazed® dozens for just 89 cents is our way of saying thank you to the guests who make Krispy Kreme part of their celebrations, traditions and everyday sweet moments."

articles.headline: Chicken Finger Favorite Zaxbys Announces Further Expansion In Chicago Suburbs
articles.author: Rick West
articles.summary: Zaxbys, the popular fast-casual chicken chain from Georgia, announced its second Chicago-area development deal as it looks to expand its footprint in the Midwest. The seven-restaurant development deal comes six months after the brand opened its first Illinois location at 13429 Illinois Route 59 in Plainfield in January. “The response we've seen in Chicagoland has validated what we believed about the market from the beginning,” said Russ Holland, chief development officer at Zaxbys, in a news release. “Between the success of our Plainfield opening and the strong interest from experienced operators, we're building meaningful momentum in the region. This second development agreement is another important step in establishing Zaxbys as a long-term growth brand throughout Chicago and the broader Midwest.”

articles.headline: KFC Is Bringing Back A Beloved Menu Favorite Fans Have Missed For Years
articles.author: Kait Hanson
articles.summary: For more than three years, KFC fans have been mourning the loss of a bite-sized favorite. They took to Reddit, social media, and even started petitions to make their voices heard, and now, the fried chicken chain is finally bringing back the beloved menu item. That’s right, starting Monday, July 13, and only while supplies last, KFC fans can once again order Popcorn Chicken. In a release shared with Southern Living, KFC confirmed that Popcorn Chicken will be available in three different ways, giving fans plenty of options to get their fix: $10 Popcorn Chicken Bucket: For those who want to skip the sides and go all-in, this option includes 16 ounces of Popcorn Chicken served with four dipping sauces. Popcorn Chicken Big Box: This combo includes six ounces of Popcorn Chicken, two Original Recipe Tenders, a medium order of fries, a medium drink, two dipping sauces, and a chocolate chunk cookie for $10.99. Popcorn Chicken Combo: This option includes six ounces of Popcorn Chicken, a medium order of fries, a medium drink, and one dipping sauce for $8.49.

articles.headline: KFC Expands In Arizona With New Sun City Restaurant
articles.summary: KFC is officially expanding in the Grand Canyon State with the opening of its newest restaurant in Sun City, Arizona. The restaurant will officially open for business on Wednesday, July 8, at 10 a.m., bringing KFC's signature fried chicken, sandwiches, bowls and family meals to the community. Located at 10801 W. Grand Avenue near Plaza de Grande, the restaurant is owned and operated by Pak Foods, LLC, a multi-unit, multi-brand operating group with locations across the Southwest. Leadership team members Tanweer and Rehman Ahmed are spearheading operations in Sun City. The opening expands Pak Foods' footprint in Arizona while further strengthening KFC's presence throughout the Greater Phoenix area.

articles.headline: Taco Bell To Replace KFC In Landrum
articles.author: Jamie Lewis
articles.summary: A Taco Bell will soon be opening in Landrum, replacing the former KFC restaurant on Highway 14 and adding another national fast-food option for area residents. Online job postings from franchise operator Fulenwider Enterprises show the company is hiring managers and crew members for the new Taco Bell at 1708 Highway 14, the site previously occupied by KFC. The KFC has ceased operations. Fulenwider Enterprises operates both KFC and Taco Bell restaurants throughout the Carolinas. While the company has not announced an official opening date, preparations for the conversion are underway.

articles.headline: CDC, FDA Link Taco Bell Lettuce Supplier To Multistate Cyclosporiasis Outbreak
articles.author: Lena H. Sun, Rachel Roubein
articles.summary: Federal health officials said Thursday that a multistate outbreak of cyclosporiasis that has sickened thousands of people has been linked to shredded iceberg lettuce served at Taco Bell restaurants in five states, confirming key findings first reported by The Washington Post.

articles.headline: Wendy's Is Selling $1 Frostys. How, When To Get One
articles.author: Greta Cross
articles.summary: Wendy's is celebrating the summer heat with $1 Frostys. To celebrate National Ice Cream Day on Sunday, July 19, Wendy's is offering $1 Frostys to rewards members who order through the mobile app, according to a news release. The deal will then be available to all customers through most of August. Several chains are offering special deals for National Ice Cream Day. Dairy Queen is serving up a free Dilly Bar with any purchase of $1 or more through the DQ app placed July 13-19. Baskin-Robbins is offering $5 off an order of $20 or more to reward members July 19-25. And delivery services like DoorDash and GoPuff have several sweet treat deals for the "holiday."

articles.headline: Chipotle Puts A New Twist On Soccer's Hydration Break With $1 Million In Free Burritos
articles.summary: Chipotle Mexican Grill today announced that it is turning one of soccer's newest matchday traditions into a $1 million burrito giveaway inspired by the long-running social conversation surrounding its iconic water cups. During the international tournament final on July 19, viewers at home can participate in a Chipotle "Water" Break for a chance to unlock free entrée offers. When play pauses for the match's second official hydration break, Chipotle will bring a stadium-inspired game to fans watching from home. The brand will share a video from the @ChipotleOpens in new window Instagram account challenging viewers to follow a lemonade-filled cup as three identical Chipotle water cups shuffle across the screen. At the end of the video, the lemonade-filled cup will reveal a text-to-win code. The first 100,000 participants to text the code to 888-222 will receive a free entrée offer.

articles.headline: 13 Of The Best New Fast Food Items Of Summer 2026 So Far
articles.author: Ian Fortey
articles.summary: Summer 2026 is in full swing, and many fast food restaurants have rolled out new seasonal offerings. The focus is on nostalgia, big and bold flavors, and reliable combinations folks know and love. There are also a few collaborative and cross-promotional efforts that have resonated with diners, proving that creative partnerships can pay off. Burger King kicked off the summer in June with a few new releases, but the one that really resonated with fans most was the Orange Dreamsicle Freezee King. Featuring a frozen orange slushy base and an orange cream cold foam topping, it's basically an orange Creamsicle turned into a drink. The Firecracker Cookie Pie is like a cross between a cheesecake and a cookie, covered with patriotic sprinkles. This summer, Wendy's went all in for its collaboration with Illumination for the film "Minions & Monsters" to bring the Banana Swirl Frosty. The base is a traditional vanilla Frosty, but it's swirled with a sweet banana sauce. You can always count on Taco Bell for some innovations that hit with fans, and the Fajita Street Chalupa seems like another winner.

articles.headline: Capacity And Foodbridge Team Up To Rewire Hunger Relief With AI
articles.summary: Food deserts are widening, donations are shrinking, and demand keeps climbing. For the hunger relief organizations caught in the middle, the math has never been harder—and the systems they rely on have never felt more disconnected. That is the problem a new St. Louis–born partnership is aiming squarely at. On July 14, 2026, Capacity, the AI-powered support automation platform, announced a partnership with FoodBridge to launch the Hunger Relief & Social Equity Initiative—a joint effort to make it dramatically easier for individuals to connect to food, benefits and community care. The pitch is deceptively simple: stitch together the pieces that have always lived in separate silos. The initiative pairs conversational, agentic and relational AI with modern food access tools so food banks and community organizations can move people from intake to a full grocery cart without the usual bureaucratic maze.

articles.headline: Building A Restaurant Telephony AI Host With Amazon Bedrock Agentcore And Amazon Nova 2 Sonic
articles.author: Sergio Barraza, Salman Ahmed, Ravi Kumar
articles.summary: Restaurants miss an average of 150 phone calls per location every month, and about 60 percent of those are customers trying to place an order or book a table. Most of these calls come in during dinner service, exactly when the host is seating guests, servers are turning tables, and the phone becomes an afterthought. Pulling someone off the floor to answer doesn’t fix the problem. It only makes two experiences worse. Adding an app or a website helps customers who prefer to order online, but it does nothing for the person who only wants to call.

articles.headline: Best Hotel Restaurants Of 2026: OpenTable List Goes Live As AI Concierge Moves To Homepage
articles.author: Maxwell Templeton
articles.summary: OpenTable on Tuesday published its 2026 Top 100 Hotel Restaurants in America — a ranking drawn from more than 10 million verified diner reviews — and simultaneously upgraded its generative AI dining assistant, Concierge, to operate directly from the platform's homepage for the first time, as announced in the company's official press release. The double announcement marks OpenTable's most aggressive move yet to position itself not just as a reservation management tool, but as the AI discovery layer that intercepts the "where should I eat?" query before travelers ever reach a search engine or review site. The timing is not coincidental. Survey data commissioned by OpenTable found that 61% of Americans who have dined at a hotel restaurant in the past five years have chosen a travel destination specifically because of its food or restaurant scene, and 60% of respondents planning summer travel say they booked a hotel because of its on-site restaurant, according to the Ripple Research survey. Those numbers show up in the platform's own reservations data: hotel restaurant dining by travelers rose 13% year-over-year, and overall seated diners at hotel restaurants climbed 7%, both measured against the same January-through-April window from 2025 to 2026.

articles.headline: Not Popeyes, Not KFC: This Chain Biscuit Was Named The Best Fast Food Side In The US
articles.author: Rolland Judd
articles.summary: Sometimes a fast food restaurant's side dishes are just as (if not more) appealing than its entrees, like McDonald's perfectly cut fries and Popeyes' Homestyle Mac and Cheese. KFC's best side dish may be its flaky biscuits, but that doesn't mean it makes the best rendition of that side across all fast food chains in the country. That distinction has been awarded to Church's Texas Chicken by USA TODAY readers, who gave the chain's Honey-Butter Biscuits top marks in USA TODAY's 10BEST fast food poll. Church's sells millions of its cherished Honey-Butter Biscuits each year, and it's easy to see why. The soft, flaky side dish is dripping with home-cooked allure because it's made from scratch daily. Once they're drizzled with the chain's iconic honey butter, they become a sweet, fluffy delicacy that customers all over the country adore. Some people are so enamored with the biscuits that they try to recreate the side dish at home, as demonstrated by the dozens of copycat recipes online.

articles.headline: Restaurant Review: Worth The Wait? Raising Cane’s Westwood Location Packs Flavor Into Every Dollar
articles.author: Emily Berkowitz
articles.summary: For three years, Bruins walked past construction barriers. Now, they’re walking out with Box Combos. As of Monday, Raising Cane’s Chicken Fingers, located on the corner of Broxton Avenue and Weyburn Avenue in Westwood, is open for business. The fast-food chain, known for its chicken fingers, Texas toast and crinkle-cut fries, operates nearly 1,000 locations across the United States. With doors finally open, the Daily Bruin tried Cane’s to see if its long-anticipated arrival lived up to the hype – and how it measured up against Westwood’s established favorites. Stepping inside Raising Cane’s on opening week felt like stepping into a celebration: three oversized disco balls twirled overhead, Mariah Carey’s “Fantasy” blasted through the speakers and every table was packed with groups of friends digging into chicken fingers and fries. The lively atmosphere felt fitting for one of Westwood’s most anticipated restaurant openings in recent history.

articles.headline: Sweetgreen Falls On Parasite Outbreak Tied To Produce
articles.author: Peyton Forte, Janet Freund
articles.summary: Sweetgreen Inc. has lost more than a quarter of its market value in just four days as investors dump the stock due to fears about outbreak of cyclosporiasis — an intestinal infection caused by parasites in raw produce and water that has sickened thousands across the US. The shares are down 26% this week after four straight sessions in the red, putting them on track for their worst week since May 2025. And they aren’t far from their record low, after falling as much as 8.5% on Thursday and closing at the weakest level in three months. It’s the worst performer in the Russell 2000 Restaurants and Bars Index over the past four sessions, which itself is down less than 1%.

articles.headline: Chick-Fil-A Construction Plans Advance In Collierville
articles.author: Stuart Rucker
articles.summary: Collierville town leaders have put an end to the debate over a new Chick-Fil-A. On Thursday, the board of mayor and aldermen approved plans for Chick-Fil-A to move forward at a new location near Poplar Avenue and Bray Station. Passionate community members were present, both supporting and opposing the new location. Some attendees expressed frustration over the abrupt decision to hold the meeting and voiced worries about the lengthy battle the town has faced regarding this issue. “Be fully on message friends, our town is for sale to the highest bidder and there is no longer anyone at the gates to protect you and your families,” a community member said.

articles.headline: Bojangles Coming To Corpus Christi, First Coastal Bend Location Expected In Spring 2027
articles.author: Ryan Hopper
articles.summary: Bojangles is bringing its first Coastal Bend location to Corpus Christi, with the restaurant expected to open in spring 2027 and create between 50 and 75 jobs. Franchise owner Terrence Thames said the Corpus Christi restaurant will be his sixth Bojangles location. Known for its Southern-style menu, Bojangles serves hand-breaded chicken, made-from-scratch biscuits and breakfast all day. "It's a Southern flavor mix," Thames said. "Our chicken tenders are hand breaded every day, fresh, handmade. Our biscuits are made every 20 minutes by hand." He believes the same demand that has fueled his San Antonio restaurants will translate to the Coastal Bend.

articles.headline: Are Hardee's Closing? What To Know After This Franchisee's Bankruptcy.
articles.author: Saleen Martin
articles.summary: A Hardee’s franchisee with more than a dozen locations in Kentucky has filed for bankruptcy after closing more than 30 locations last year. Hardee’s franchisee Superior Star LLC filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy on Thursday, July 9, according to court documents reviewed by USA TODAY. The company said it owes more than 1,000 creditors and has between $10 million and $50 million in assets and liabilities. As of July, the company operates 59 Hardee’s locations across 10 states, including 14 in Kentucky, according to the documents. While the company generated $80 million in gross revenue in 2025, the franchisee said it still wasn’t enough for the owners to have a profitable year. Superior Star isn't the only franchisee to file for bankruptcy. Franchisee ⁠ARC Burger filed in 2025, citing financial difficulties, and closed 77 locations, reported the Tallahassee Democrat, part of the USA TODAY Network. Hardee's has since reopened some of those closed locations in Georgia, South Carolina and Missouri, noting that they are “now corporate owned."

articles.headline: Why KFC Stopped Calling Itself Kentucky Fried Chicken — And The Myths That Still Won't Die
articles.author: Teresa Mull
articles.summary: Kentucky Fried Chicken officially shortened its name to KFC in 1991, and the reason behind the rebranding has fueled rumors ever since. One persistent myth falsely claims the Commonwealth of Kentucky trademarked the state's name and forced the company to pay licensing fees. Another urban legend alleged KFC was breeding extra-large, "genetically modified organisms" with extra legs, so they couldn’t technically call their signature dish "chicken" anymore. The mutant chicken rumors resulted in Chinese courts finding several companies liable for spreading false claims that damaged KFC’s reputation. KFC China won a defamation lawsuit that resulted in three tech firms being fined for damaging KFC’s brand, Reuters reported in 2016.

articles.headline: 10 Clear Signs KFC's Struggles Aren't Over
articles.author: Fred Decker
articles.summary: KFC is considered to be one of the most iconic American fast food brands, with its distinctive red and white buckets of chicken and familiar "It's finger lickin' good" slogan. The company has been around in one way or another since Colonel Harland Sanders opened Sanders Court and Café in Corbin, Kentucky in 1930. After more than two decades of trials and travails – including perfecting his now-famous recipe — Sanders turned to franchising. The first Kentucky Fried Chicken franchise opened in 1952, and rapidly grew into a titan of the then-new fast food industry.

articles.headline: KFC Expands In Arizona With Restaurant Opening In Sun City
articles.summary: KFC has expanded its presence in Arizona with the opening of a restaurant in Sun City, according to a press release. The restaurant, located at 10801 W. Grand Ave. near Plaza de Grande, officially opened for business on July 8. The location is owned and operated by Pak Foods, LLC, a Houston-based franchisee group that operates more than 160 restaurants across the Southwest. Leadership team members Tanweer and Rehman Ahmed are spearheading the operations in Sun City. "We're proud to officially bring KFC to the Sun City community and create a convenient new destination for residents and visitors to enjoy the brand's iconic menu offerings," Rehman said in the press release. "Our team is committed to providing a welcoming experience and high-quality service for every guest who visits. We look forward to becoming part of the community and serving Sun City residents for years to come."

articles.headline: 5 Chain Restaurants With The Best Loaded Mashed Potato Bowls, According To Diners
articles.author: Leah Groth
articles.summary: The potato is one of the most versatile veggies. You can bake it, mash it, fry it; the options are endless. Mashed potatoes are a favorite, especially when loaded. Some places serve it in a bowl as a “loaded mashed potato” side, while others add fried chicken and veggies and serve it as an entree. The most famous restaurant serving loaded mashed potato bowls is KFC, which offers the signature Famous Bowl made with mashed potatoes, gravy, sweet corn, crispy chicken nuggets, and a 3-cheese blend. They are so legendary that there are endless copycat recipes available online.

articles.headline: Wendy’s Is Celebrating National Ice Cream Day With $1 Frostys
articles.author: Mikaela Hardiman
articles.summary: Those days have mostly disappeared. Dollar menus became value menus, value menus became $5 meal deals, and now even a basic combo can creep toward $15 depending on where you live. For a little while, though, Wendy’s is bringing back one of fast food’s increasingly rare bargains. To celebrate National Ice Cream Day, the chain is offering small Classic Chocolate and Vanilla Frostys for just $1. Wendy’s Rewards members get early access through the app on July 19th, before the promotion opens to everyone from July 20th through August 23rd at participating locations. It’s a straightforward deal. But it also feels like a reminder that the Frosty has become one of fast food’s few untouchable classics.

articles.headline: World Cup Final 2026 Deals And Freebies: Mcdonald’s, Chipotle And More
articles.author: Andrea Palladino
articles.summary: As the World Cup Final kicks off on Sunday, July 19, fans don’t need a jersey to score and win big. Whether you’re a die-hard Soccer fan or just looking for halftime snacks, restaurants and chains are serving up major league deals for your watch party. The Mexican chain is giving away $1 million in free food during Sunday’s final. Keep your eyes peeled during the match’s second hydration break, usually the 67th-minute mark.Chipotle will post an instagram video that will reveal a code to help you win free food.

articles.headline: This Is The Worst Week In Slop Bowl History
articles.author: Nitish Pahwa
articles.summary: We still don’t know what exactly is driving our national outbreak of cyclosporiasis, the parasitic infection that has stricken thousands of Americans with explosive diarrhea. In part, you can thank the Trump administration’s self-sabotaged public health apparatus. That likely includes Taco Bell—which has now “voluntarily and temporarily” removed some unnamed ingredients from restaurants located within various Midwestern and Appalachian states—but none of this is a mere consequence of living más. The sheer reach of the Cyclospora parasite indicates other contributors at play, and despite expert recommendations to the contrary, it’s already fomenting a mild panic over produce. Shares of publicly traded fast-casual staples like Sweetgreen, Cava, and Chipotle took steady hits this week, as did T-Bell parent company Yum! Brands. And sheer uncertainty abounds over how to handle raw fruits and veggies while this parasite crawls about.

articles.headline: How Burger King Is Preparing For The GLP-1 Revolution
articles.author: Grace Thomas, Hallie Jackson
articles.summary: As weight loss drugs reshape how Americans eat, Burger King is gearing up for a future where customers still want fast food — just less of it. “My impulse has always been that this GLP-1 movement is going to have a profound impact on the industry,” said Burger King’s president of U.S. and Canada, Tom Curtis, in an interview in Miami for NBC’s Business in America franchise. “We haven’t seen it yet, so I keep telling the team, ‘Hey, we need to be prepared for this,’” he said. At Burger King, the preparations have already begun. Curtis said the chain is testing menu items like Whopper Bites and protein-forward bowls, partly to accommodate the shifting tastes of GLP-1 users.

articles.headline: How Burger King Is Navigating Changing American Eating Habits And Rising Costs
articles.summary: NBC News’ Hallie Jackson sat down with Burger King President Tom Curtis to discuss how his company, famous for its Whopper burgers, is positioning itself at a time when more and more Americans turn to GLP-1s to lose weight.

articles.headline: Burger King Offers Duo And Trio Meal Deals — Starting At Just $5
articles.summary: Would like two items or three? Burger King is treating bargain hunters like royalty with two great meal deal options – the Duo or Trio. You have the option of choosing items from a select menu for a discounted price: Two items for $5 or three items for $7. Create your very own value meal deal or share the savings with a friend, family or co-worker. Plus, you can always save the extra item(s) for dinner or lunch the next day…or even a midnight snack!

articles.headline: Over 2,000 Operators Surveyed In New Drive-Thru Study
articles.summary: Quick-service restaurant operators want a seamless, profitable drive-thru. Legacy in-ground sensors and operational blind spots quietly leak revenue from the lane without anyone realizing it. The alternative gaining rapid traction is camera-based AI and video analytics. Unlike in-ground loops, camera-based AI technology provides “visual truth” by tracking the total journey from the moment a car enters the property. The report highlights that 55.4 percent of operators who successfully solved lane issues did so through technology and AI adoption, while only 12.1 percent found success through staffing adjustments alone.

articles.headline: The Restaurant Profitability Gap: Why AI Adopters Are Pulling Ahead
articles.summary: For years, "AI in the back office" sounded like a pitch deck promise. New data suggests it's now a P&L reality—and the operators who ignored it may be watching competitors quietly widen the margin gap. On July 16, 2026, Restaurant365, the restaurant management platform, released its 2026 State of the Restaurant Industry Mid-Year Report, drawing on survey responses from more than 420 restaurant operators representing nearly 10,000 U.S. locations across quick-service, fast casual, casual dining, fine dining, pizza, and coffee concepts. The headline finding: a measurable Restaurant Profitability Gap is opening between operators who use AI to turn operational data into action and those who haven't adopted it yet. According to the report, 62% of operators have implemented or plan to implement AI in at least one back-office function—more than double the level reported at the beginning of the year. Reporting and analytics lead the way, followed by scheduling and inventory forecasting.

articles.headline: 4 Ways AI-Using Restaurants Are Cutting Food, Labor Costs To Outpace Competitors
articles.author: Cherryh Cansler
articles.summary: Let's start with the obvious: Restaurants using AI are pulling ahead of those that aren't. If that headline alone doesn't shock you, I'm not surprised. We've been watching this trend build for a while now, but mid-year research from Restaurant365 puts real numbers behind the gap. Its 2026 State of the Restaurant Industry Mid-Year Report surveyed over 420 operators representing nearly 10,000 U.S. locations spanning quick-service, fast casual, casual dining, fine dining, pizza and coffee concepts. The company is calling the divide it uncovered the "Restaurant Profitability Gap," and it's not just a catchy label; it's a measurable difference in how restaurants are performing depending on whether they've put AI to work in the back office.

articles.headline: Panera Just Announced A Big Change, And Now Some Fans Are Swearing Off The Chain For Good
articles.author: Samantha Dillard
articles.summary: Panera makes a habit of listening to its guests. When it entered its “new era,” it began rebuilding the menu with customer value and preferences in mind. Over the last few years, fans have been treated to revamped versions of their favorite meals, a new value menu, and the return of beloved seasonal favorites. But not every change is being met with excitement. Recently, Panera announced an update to its Sip Club membership, and some fans say they’re considering leaving the chain for good.

articles.headline: My Favorite Fast Food Dessert Is So Much Better Than A Dairy Queen Blizzard—Here's Why
articles.author: Deanne Revel
articles.summary: Soft serve is the OG fast food dessert. Without drive-thru soft serve, we wouldn't have the Wendy's Frosty, A&W's Root Beer Float, or the Dairy Queen Blizzard. DQ's iconic treat has come a long way since the '80s, with seemingly an infinite list of flavor options and secret menu items to boot. But I get overwhelmed by a vast menu. And I'm also a creature of habit. Nothing is better than a classic Oreo Blizzard. Well, except for the Oreo McFlurry. Turn your cup upside down all you want. To quote the modern-day sage Shania Twain, "That don't impress me much." The McDonald's Oreo McFlurry is ten times better than the Dairy Queen Oreo Blizzard—and I can prove it.

articles.headline: Surprise, Raising Cane's Reopens In Fort Collins Earlier Than Expected
articles.author: David Dishman
articles.summary: Raising Cane's has reopened in Fort Collins. The popular national fried chicken chain opened July 18 after a summer renovation. Founded in Baton Rouge, Louisiana, in 1996, Raising Cane's is known for its chicken fingers, crinkle-cut fries, Texas toast and signature Cane's Sauce. The chain has since grown into one of the nation's largest chicken-focused fast-food brands, with more than 800 restaurants nationwide, according to the company's website.

articles.headline: Raising Cane's Owner Todd Graves On How Viral Tom Brady-Rob Gronkowski Dunk Tank Came To Be At Fanatics Fest
articles.author: Scott Thompson
articles.summary: Fanatics Fest is a viral moment waiting to happen considering the plethora of superstars under one Manhattan roof, and perhaps the first one came on Thursday’s opening day at the Javits Center in the Raising Cane’s activation. It was a legendary combination of Tom Brady, Rob Gronkowski and 500 gallons of Raising Cane’s famous sauce filled in a dunk tank. Brady, whose accuracy with a football is still unmatched, didn’t miss his target. Gronkowski went splashing into the sauce, as Brady and Raising Cane’s owner Todd Graves joined the masses in celebrating one of the coolest moments to kick off Fanatics Fest.

articles.headline: The Fast Food Spicy Chicken Sandwich You Should Order Every Time
articles.author: Nikita Ephanov
articles.summary: Americans love eating chicken; it comes as no surprise the protein is such a fast food staple. Convenient poultry options range from fried tenders to grilled filets, but an especially tasty dish is a spicy chicken sandwich. The delights the dish are widespread, so to navigate the options, our own Food Republic reviewer ranked 7 fast food spicy chicken sandwiches. The option that leads the pack? Unsurprising to many, the Chick-fil-A spicy chicken sandwich. The most successful chicken chain gets the details right, covering a tender marinated chicken breast in a signature paprika-cayenne seasoning, pressure fried in peanut oil to perfection. The battered chicken is served on buttery buns, alongside a few pickle slices, all ready to dip into the chain's expansive selection of sauces.

articles.headline: South Main Street KFC Closes Out Of Nowhere, With Sign Sending Customers To Other Locations
articles.author: Ricardo Tovar
articles.summary: A South Main fast-food chain location has seemingly closed out of nowhere, leaving residents stumped about what happened. The Kentucky Fried Chicken on South Main Street has closed, with a sign directing customers to the chain’s other two locations in town. The other two locations are at 700 East Alisal Street and 2040 North Main Street. The South Main location had decent reviews, with 3.1 out of 5 stars based on 345 reviews. When asked about it, an employee at another Salinas location said the closure was sudden and shocking, and that they found out the same day the location closed on Saturday.

articles.headline: Humorous Fast-Food Campaigns
articles.author: Amy Duong
articles.summary: The KFC World Cup campaign reimagines the widely shared 2007 photograph of Lionel Messi bathing baby Lamine Yamal by replacing Messi with Colonel Sanders and transforming Yamal into a miniature Ronald McDonald. Shared ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup final, the social media post was captioned "Y'all my sons fr," extending the parody into a playful jab at rival fast-food chain McDonald's. The campaign coincided with renewed attention surrounding the original image as Spain and Argentina prepared to meet in the tournament's final. The photograph was originally taken by Joan Monfort for a UNICEF charity calendar organized by Spanish newspaper Sport before resurfacing during UEFA Euro 2024. KFC's use of Ronald McDonald also referenced Yamal's appearance in McDonald's FIFA World Cup 26 campaign alongside David Beckham, Ronaldinho, Thierry Henry, and Son Heung-min.

articles.headline: Popular Chicken Menu Relaunches
articles.author: Kalin Ned
articles.summary: Kentucky Fried Chicken has officially brought back its Popcorn Chicken to menus nationwide after more than three years of sustained consumer demand. While the item initially disappeared from the lineup in 2023, KFC noted that relentless fan feedback through comments, direct messages, and online petitions was the driving force behind the decision to bring it back. The Popcorn Chicken first introduced in the early 1990s, is described as a crunchy, snackable bite-sized chicken offering, and is available in three configurations — a 16-ounce bucket designed for sharing at $10, a Big Box combination including tenders, fries, a drink, and a cookie for $10.99, and a combo meal with fries and a drink for $8.49. The brand has also partnered with GIPHY to release a collection of exclusive Popcorn Chicken GIFs featuring the Colonel.

articles.headline: Taco Bell Traffic Sinks After Lettuce Tied To Parasite Cases
articles.author: Daniela Sirtori
articles.summary: Visits to Taco Bell locations have plummeted following a parasite outbreak that authorities linked to lettuce sold by one of the company’s suppliers. On July 17, foot traffic was almost 19% below Taco Bell’s daily average for Fridays between Jan. 1 and July 6, according to Placer.ai, which tracks activity from mobile devices to show trends in customer visits. The decline was far greater than the overall 1.9% drop registered by the fast-food category that day.

articles.headline: Wendy's Launches Sonic The Hedgehog Kids’ Meal For 35th Anniversary
articles.author: Drew Pittock
articles.summary: Everyone’s favorite fast-running, mischievous, coin-collecting hedgehog is turning 35. To mark the occasion, Wendy’s is launching a new kids’ meal experience. Fans of Sonic the Hedgehog will have the opportunity to collect seven character card cases, with a total of 35 cards in all. Here’s what you should know about the partnership between Sonic and Wendy’s. Wendy’s announced July 20 in a news release that now through early September, the kids’ meal experience at participating locations will celebrate the 35th anniversary of Sonic the Hedgehog.

articles.headline: Wingstop Added More Restaurants Than Any US Chain In 2025, Report Says
articles.author: Fernando Cervantes Jr.
articles.summary: Americans can't seem to get enough Wingstop. The Texas-based chicken chain opened more new restaurants than any other U.S. chain in 2025, adding 382 locations and beating out major brands including Chipotle, Starbucks, Chick-fil-A and Taco Bell, according to a new ranking from QSR Magazine. Wingstop's first-place ranking comes as the chicken chain continues its rapid expansion. According to QSR Magazine, Wingstop had 785 locations when it went public in 2015. By the end of fiscal 2025, the company operated 3,056 restaurants worldwide.

articles.headline: Burger King Unveils 'Whopper Guarantee' With Free Burger If Order Misses The Mark
articles.author: Bonny Chu
articles.summary: Burger King is making a major push to improve customer satisfaction, announcing Monday that it will remake any unsatisfactory Whopper for free and offer guests a complimentary Whopper on their next visit. The fast-food chain said the "Whopper Guarantee" initiative will launch alongside a new team of employees called "Your Way Champions," who will be specifically dedicated to addressing customer needs rather than solely overseeing restaurant operations. "Burger King is introducing two new initiatives designed to improve the moments Guests told the brand matter most – providing a consistent, accurate and welcoming in-restaurant experience – by introducing Your Way Champions and the Whopper Guarantee," the Florida-based chain said.

articles.headline: Have It Your Way: Beyond The Burger King Ingredient Scare
articles.source: American Council on Science and Health
articles.author: Chuck Dinerstein
articles.summary: A viral video criticizing Burger King’s ingredient makes for compelling entertainment, but how well do its scientific claims hold up? Beyond the inflammatory language, the evidence paints a more nuanced picture—one in which food additives, ultra-processed foods, convenience, and economics all play important roles. Understanding that balance may be more valuable than simply counting ingredients. It is true that a single menu item can contain a multiplicity of ingredients associated with ultra-processed foods, whatever they might be. Those ingredients provide a uniform texture and taste while enhancing shelf stability for long supply chains. They rarely add nutritional value. Of course, an absolute count of ingredients tells us little.

articles.headline: AI In Restaurant Kitchens: What To Automate, What To Keep Human
articles.author: Paulina Schmidt, Thomas Bissegger
articles.summary: The question of whether to use AI in restaurant kitchens is hardly up for debate anymore, because the hospitality industry is already experimenting with these tools, from forecasting and workflow optimization to menu development. Projects such as the Restaurant “WOOHOO” in Dubai are catching the industry's attention as they are using a large-scale culinary language model, an AI chef called “Chef Aiman” that has been trained on decades of food science research, molecular composition data, and thousands of recipes from cooking traditions around the world, helping to design people's menus.

articles.headline: How Much Is Invisible Waste Costing Your Restaurant?
articles.summary: While most operators fret about increasing foot traffic and check size, there may be a surprising factor killing profits. And that’s restaurant waste. While generic AI can spot patterns in data, it doesn’t understand that a seasonal cocktail menu change affects inventory turns across six categories, or that a substitution from your produce vendor changes your plate cost even if the invoice looks similar. Restaurant operations require context, not just pattern recognition. The benefit of restaurant-specific AI trained on hospitality operational data is that it understands how these moves affect purchasing, recipes and margins. Craftable’s AI layer, dubbed “Crafty,” goes further by connecting inventory data directly to recipes. For example, it can recommend specials that use ingredients already on hand or flag how a seemingly innocuous vendor substitution elevates the cost per plate. “To allow for that kind of proactive identification, the AI must understand what the data means in a restaurant context, not just what the numbers say,” Cantu says.

articles.headline: Restaurant Operators Turn To Training And Tech To Meet Cost Challenges
articles.author: Matthew Liedke
articles.summary: Restaurant and foodservice sales are projected to reach $1.55 trillion in 2026, a 4.8 percent increase from 2025. The forecast from the National Restaurant Association earlier this year paints a promising picture for the industry. The path to getting there, however, is coming with its share of hurdles for restaurant operators facing ongoing challenges. Midway through the year, Restaurant365 surveyed more than 420 operators to understand the issues they’re facing. Artificial intelligence remains a lower priority, meanwhile, with 9.1 percent investing in back of house AI applications and 6.3 percent looking toward front-of-house upgrades. “I would say for the most part, operators are a little bit slower to adopt it, but that doesn’t mean they’re afraid of it,” Joe Hannon, Restaurant365 general manager of inventory and sales said. “That just means they’re either holding out or asking if it’s going to help their ROI or move their margins. When it comes to AI, the gap is going to be between those who act on it and those who don’t. The uptick in folks who’re adopting our AI Tools is a slow burn to build trust and prove value.”

articles.headline: Loman AI Has Joined The Toast Partner Ecosystem For Restaurants
articles.author: Adam Harrie
articles.summary: Loman AI has joined the Toast Partner Ecosystem, enabling restaurants to use Loman's AI phone-answering platform to take orders, book reservations and answer callers' questions around the clock. Through the Loman AI Toast integration, phone orders flow directly into existing Toast workflows, eliminating the need for extra tablets, manual re-entry or pulling staff off the line to handle calls during rush periods. Loman is already live in more than 1,500 restaurants, with the strongest adoption among high-call-volume concepts such as pizza, wings and takeout-driven businesses, where missed calls often translate into missed orders. Loman AI shows how AI-powered phone systems are becoming essential restaurant infrastructure by closing the gap between customer calls and kitchen execution during peak hours.

articles.headline: NKY Chick-Fil-A’s To Hold 13th Annual Stuff The Bus Challenge For School Supplies
articles.summary: Northern Kentucky Chick-fil-A restaurants will hold their 13th annual “Stuff The Bus Challenge” to provide supplies to area schools on Thursday, July 23 from 11 a.m.–7 p.m. Fort Wright, Houston Road, Mall Road, Newport Pavilion, and Union Promenade Chick-fil-A Restaurants are accepting donations for all school districts. Northern Kentucky community members who donate five or more school supplies from the list will receive a coupon for a free Chick-fil-A Original Chicken Sandwich. The Stuff The Bus Challenge demonstrates how Chick-fil-A cares for its local communities in a fun engaging way that gives students a solid foundation for the school year.

articles.headline: McDonald's Adds New Breakfast Biscuit To Menus, But Not For Long
articles.summary: McDonald's has a new sweet and savory breakfast item hitting menus this week, but it's only available for a limited time. Starting Tuesday, McDonald's will debut a Honey Brown Butter Bacon, Egg & Cheese Biscuit to menus at participating locations nationwide, an announcement from the Chicago-based chain said. According to the announcement, the sandwich will be available at McDonald's locations in the Chicago area and in northwest Indiana. "This breakfast sandwich is the perfect spin on the classic bacon egg and cheese, taking those ingredients and nestling them between two freshly baked biscuits with creamy, toasty Honey Brown Butter," the restaurant said.

articles.headline: Howard Athletics Partners With McDonald’s
articles.summary: Howard University Department of Athletics and the local McDonald's Owner Operators across the Greater Washington, D.C., Baltimore, and Eastern Shore area unveiled a new multi-year deal that launched Wednesday, July 1. This partnership features McDonald's as an Official Corporate Sponsor of Howard Athletics, reflecting a shared commitment to Howard's student-athletes, the surrounding community and Bison Nation. "We are extremely thrilled to partner with McDonald's," said Howard Vice President of Athletics Kery Davis. "This partnership gives both parties an opportunity to impact young men and women while engaging with the community and Bison Nation. We look forward to a long, exciting partnership."

articles.headline: Popular Fried Chicken Chain Bojangles Opens First Arizona Location
articles.author: Sara Crocker
articles.summary: It’s officially “Bo’ Time” for fried chicken and biscuits in the Valley. After pushing off its opening a week, Bojangles, the popular Carolina fast food joint, is now open in Gilbert. Bojangles debut Arizona store, at Val Vista Drive and Melrose Street, started serving diners at 5 a.m. Customers who visit today can enter for a chance to win a year of free breakfast. “We are ecstatic about introducing Bojangles to Phoenix and bringing our unique Southern charm to the market,” Jeanette Davis, a senior vice president of franchise operator LV Petroleum, said in a press release. In March 2024, Bojangles announced plans to expand into Arizona, with 20 restaurants anticipated to open across the Valley. The chain started in 1977 in Charlotte, North Carolina, and has grown to include 880 restaurants in 24 states.

articles.headline: Jersey Mike's Tops Chick-Fil-A – But The Rankings Tell A Bigger Story
articles.author: Mary Walrath-Holdridge
articles.summary: Chick-fil-A has been at the top of customer satisfaction rankings for over a decade, so how was it finally knocked off the fast food throne? This year, things went Jersey Mike’s way, ending Chick-fil-A’s 11-year run at No. 1 in the quick-service category of the American Customer Satisfaction Index. But that doesn’t mean Chick-fil-A’s ratings suddenly fell off – in fact, they didn't fall at all. According to the ACSI data, which measures how diners rate major restaurant chains, Chick-fil-A’s score stayed the same as the previous survey, while Jersey Mike’s moved slightly ahead. For customers, the bigger takeaway is that competition in fast food appears to be getting tighter, especially as hungry patrons pay closer attention to freshness, variety and value.

articles.headline: Why Domino’s Sees Order Counts As Its Primary Growth Engine
articles.summary: The pizza giant delivered a second quarter that looked mixed on paper. U.S. same-store sales rose just 0.1 percent, weighed down by a lower average ticket after a premium menu campaign failed to resonate with customers. But Domino’s believes the underlying health of the business is considerably stronger than that figure suggests. Throughout what marked Russell Weiner’s final earnings call as CEO, he repeatedly returned to one message, which is that Domino’s is winning more customers, taking market share, and building a larger customer base that should fuel future sales, unit growth, and franchise profitability.

articles.headline: KFC Shuts Down Hundreds Of U.S. Restaurants: What Do The Numbers Mean?
articles.author: Jessica Jones-Gorman
articles.summary: KFC is shrinking its U.S. footprint and the reason for the closures is not immediately evident, according to a recent report. The global fast-food restaurant chain, famous for its signature Southern-style fried chicken, has shuttered 312 restaurants across the country in the past year, AI search visibility platform Local Falcon recently revealed — which amounts to a 7.64% reduction in stores. “In all, 278 cities lost a KFC, but just 257 of them lost only one,” the report noted. “San Antonio is the sharp exception. The Texas city lost seven KFC locations over the year, more than any other city in the country …”

articles.headline: KFC Turns The Internet's Pickle Obsession Into Nail Bar, Pop-Up And Pickle Club Menu
articles.author: Havas Paris
articles.summary: From the back of the pantry to global food phenomenon, the pickle has become impossible to ignore. Taste tests, outrageous recipes, unexpected pairings and viral content: across every continent, it has established itself as one of the most talked-about condiments on social media. Deeply divisive, the pickle sparks as much debate as it does devotion - and that's precisely what makes it so successful. Equal parts fascination, humour and creativity, it fuels conversations and has become a true cultural icon. KFC has tapped into this momentum with 'PICKLE CLUB', a new campaign from Havas that celebrates the most passionate fans of this iconic condiment.

articles.headline: Taco Bell And Chipotle Face A Problem Bigger Than Lettuce
articles.author: Daniel Kline
articles.summary: A food safety scandal can disappear from headlines within weeks or follow a restaurant brand for years. Taco Bell is now facing the same question that once challenged Chipotle: How long will customers remember? While they have not been implicated by the FDA in serving the infected lettuce that has been causing severe gastrointestinal distress, Chipotle, Cava, Chopt, Panera Bread, and other chains that offer lettuce have seen collateral damage from the scandal. And although it’s never good to be associated with explosive diarrhea, the real issue for these chains is how consumers view the incident over the long term. Past history shows that it can vary greatly.

articles.headline: Taco Bell Quietly Added 2 New Burritos To The Menu For A Limited Time
articles.author: Samantha Dillard
articles.summary: This summer, Taco Bell has been on a roll, and it shows no signs of slowing down. Last month, the chain dropped new fajitas-inspired entrées and began testing two never-before-seen chili cheese menu items in select locations. If that wasn’t enough to make you ring the bell, the restaurant also brought back the Enchirito, a sauce-topped burrito that fans have been waiting to see return for years. Now, Taco Bell has expanded its offerings once again with three new menu items that pepper Jack cheese lovers won’t want to miss.

articles.headline: Wendy's Launches Sonic The Hedgehog Kids' Meals With Trading Cards
articles.summary: To celebrate the 35th anniversary of Sonic the Hedgehog, Wendy's is offering a collectible Kids' Meal experience this summer featuring one of seven character card cases, each containing a unique set of trading cards. Available now through early September at participating U.S. restaurants, the Wendy's app and Wendys.com, the promotion allows fans to collect 35 trading cards in all. The seven character sets feature heroes like Sonic the Hedgehog, Amy Rose and Miles "Tails" Prower, alongside Silver the Hedgehog, Knuckles the Echidna, Shadow the Hedgehog and Rouge the Bat.

articles.headline: Chipotle Is Gaining Some Of Its Value Mojo Back
articles.author: Danny Klein
articles.summary: For 15th quarters, William Blair has tracked restaurant engagement and value (check out the last one here). It’s been a chaotic line at times, with guests pulling back over price and shifting toward digital options, then feeling some tech fatigue, and moving up and down the perception ladder when it comes to certain brands. Before diving into trends from the most recent edition, released this week, let’s turn to concept rankings. The percentage of respondents “eating out more often than last year” from the roughly 500 guests surveyed by William Blair outpaced those eating out less often at half the brands in its coverage zone. The gap was most favorable at Black Rock Coffee Bar, Kura Sushi, and Starbucks.

articles.headline: Burger King Launches New 'Whopper Guarantee' To Win Back Customers
articles.summary: Burger King has launched a new "Whopper Guarantee" promotion to win back customers from rivals like McDonald's. Burger King announced Monday that it is introducing two new initiatives aimed at improving customers' in-restaurant experience after receiving thousands of calls and text messages through a nationwide feedback campaign. The fast-food chain said it is launching a new "Your Way Champion" restaurant manager role and a Whopper Guarantee as part of its ongoing brand transformation.

articles.headline: Burger King Is Reimagining The Restaurant Manager Role To Improve Service
articles.author: Jonathan Maze
articles.summary: Burger King’s latest effort to improve the operation of its restaurants involves special shirts. The fast-food chain, which has spent the bulk of the past five years working to upgrade the way its restaurants are run, is reimagining the manager role, turning them into “Your Way Champions.” The champions will operate as the point person in the restaurant. They will welcome customers, ensure orders are correct and be available when things are needed. They will be marked by a special uniform, which was sent to stores two weeks ago. The champions made their debut this week.

articles.headline: QSR Evolution Launches QSR AI Connect, Bringing Restaurant Leaders Together To Shape The Future of AI
articles.summary: Artificial intelligence is quickly becoming one of the most significant forces shaping the restaurant industry. From marketing and loyalty to operations, labor, guest experience, and enterprise decision-making, restaurant leaders are facing unprecedented opportunities to rethink how their businesses grow and operate. To help restaurant executives move beyond experimentation and develop a practical path forward, the QSR Evolution Conference, run by Arrowfly and the teams behind QSR and FSR magazines, is launching QSR AI Connect, a new executive initiative designed to help leaders navigate the rapidly evolving AI landscape through education, collaboration, and practical application.

articles.headline: AI Can Drive Better Results, But It Matters What It’s Built On
articles.author: Gladys Kong
articles.summary: Speed is the part of the artificial intelligence (AI) story everyone leads with. Teams that used to wait days for analyst-generated insights can now get answers in seconds. While there is a lot of AI hype and talk about transformational change sometime down the road, we’re seeing tremendous opportunity across key verticals right now. QSR. For QSR brands, AI is accelerating decisions across site selection, operations, and competitive positioning. Teams can rank and evaluate new locations using trade area analysis, surface underperformance signals earlier, and run scenario models before committing resources, all without putting requests in a queue for the analytics team.

articles.headline: Agentic Discovery Inspires Review Generator From Marqii, Thanx
articles.author: Hans Lammeman
articles.summary: With the rise of agentic ordering and AI discovery comes increased value of restaurant reviews, a common source of information for chatbots asked about foodservice operations. That trend inspired Marqii Review Generation, a tool announced July 21 from hospitality-focused discovery platform Marqii. Launched in partnership with guest engagement software system Thanx, the feature automatically triggers SMS requests for reviews to guests immediately after they order. “I’m always looking for tools that deliver real results for my clients without adding to their workload, and Review Generation checks both boxes,” Stacey Kane, chief marketing officer, said in a release. “Asking for a review at exactly the right moment, right after a great ordering experience, is something every brand should be doing. Marqii has made it completely automatic.”

articles.headline: Popeyes Shines A Spotlight On Its Upgraded Chicken Tenders
articles.author: Alicia Kelso
articles.summary: Popeyes recently upgraded its chicken tenders and now it wants consumers to try them, kicking off a national delivery offer through July 27 in which 11 tenders, one large side, and four biscuits are available for $25 through DoorDash and Uber Eats. The same deal is available for $20 for in-store guests. July 27 is National Chicken Finger Day. The Popeyes’ culinary team has been updating the chain’s tenders to ensure consistent hand-breaded Louisiana flavor and “generous portions.” "Our Chicken Tenders have earned a loyal following for a reason. They're packed with the bold Louisiana flavor and signature crunch that only Popeyes can deliver," Chief Marketing Officer Matt Rubin said in a statement. "Whether fans are stacking their own Chicken Tender Tower at home or joining us at The Surf Lodge in Montauk, we're bringing craveable flavor to the center of one of summer's biggest cultural moments."

articles.headline: Two Belleville Hardee’s Restaurants Close Suddenly. Here’s What We Know
articles.author: Jennifer Green
articles.summary: Hardee’s restaurants at 2 S. Belt East and 2516 Green Mount Commons Drive in Belleville abruptly closed this week. An employee at the Green Mount Commons location declined to give his name, but said they learned of the closing Monday morning around 10 a.m. All signage was gone from each location by Tuesday afternoon. Multiple notices were posted on the doors and drive-thru speaker at Green Mount stating, ”This location is closed. Thank you for your business. Please visit our other location in Belleville.” The Hardee’s location at 1617 N. Belt West remains open. Other metro-east locations also remain open in O’Fallon, Caseyville, Collinsville, Glen Carbon, Granite City, and Waterloo.

articles.headline: Layne's Chicken Fingers Hits 50 Units And 24 New Franchise Deals In 2026
articles.summary: Fifty open restaurants. Two dozen new franchise agreements. A California debut in the works. Layne's Chicken Fingers didn't just have a good start to 2026 — it hit the growth inflection point that separates emerging concepts from real, scalable franchise brands. At the close of the second quarter, the Texas "Born and Breaded" chicken finger franchise has expanded to more than 50 operating restaurants and signed two dozen franchise agreements, fueled by a mix of internal development and fresh operators joining the system. The brand also landed a placement on the prestigious Franchise 500 — a credibility marker that matters when you're courting multi-unit operators and institutional capital.

articles.headline: Wingstop Added More Restaurants Than Any US Chain In 2025, Report Says
articles.author: Fernando Cervantes Jr.
articles.summary: Americans can't seem to get enough Wingstop. The Texas-based chicken chain opened more new restaurants than any other U.S. chain in 2025, adding 382 locations and beating out major brands including Chipotle, Starbucks, Chick-fil-A and Taco Bell, according to a new ranking from QSR Magazine. Wingstop's first-place ranking comes as the chicken chain continues its rapid expansion. According to QSR Magazine, Wingstop had 785 locations when it went public in 2015. By the end of fiscal 2025, the company operated 3,056 restaurants worldwide.
